title: Chapter 8 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of navigation and routing!

questions.question: What does Navigator.push do?
options: Adds a new route to the stack
questions.explanation: push adds a new route to the top of the navigation stack.

questions.question: Navigator uses a queue data structure.
questions.explanation: Navigator uses a stack (LIFO).

questions.question: To remove the current route and return to the previous, call Navigator._____.
questions.explanation: Navigator.pop removes the top route.

questions.question: Which method is used to navigate to a named route?
questions.explanation: pushNamed is the correct method for named routes.

questions.question: You can pass arguments to a named route using the 'arguments' parameter.
questions.explanation: Yes, pushNamed accepts an arguments object.

questions.question: How do you retrieve arguments in a named route?
questions.explanation: Arguments are in the route's settings.

questions.question: The return type of Navigator.push is a _____.
questions.explanation: push returns a Future that resolves with the pop result.

questions.question: You can return data from a screen by passing it to Navigator.pop.
questions.explanation: Navigator.pop(context, result) sends data back.

questions.question: Which widget is used for bottom navigation tabs?
questions.explanation: BottomNavigationBar (Material) and CupertinoTabBar (iOS) are used.

questions.question: To manage a TabBar and TabBarView, you typically use a _____.
questions.explanation: TabController synchronizes the TabBar and TabBarView.

questions.question: IndexedStack keeps all its children alive, even invisible ones.
questions.explanation: IndexedStack renders only the active child but keeps all in memory.

questions.question: What is the purpose of onGenerateRoute?
options: To generate routes dynamically, especially with arguments
questions.explanation: onGenerateRoute gives you full control to create routes based on settings.

questions.question: The initial route in a MaterialApp is set via the _____ parameter.
questions.explanation: initialRoute defines which named route to show first.

questions.question: Pushing a route with MaterialPageRoute gives a platform-appropriate transition.
questions.explanation: MaterialPageRoute provides material design transitions.

questions.question: How do you pop the current route and return to the root (first) route?
questions.explanation: popUntil with a predicate that returns true for the first route.

questions.question: To replace the current route with a new one, use Navigator._____.
questions.explanation: pushReplacement removes the current route and adds the new one.

questions.question: The back button on Android automatically calls Navigator.pop.
questions.explanation: Yes, the system back button triggers a pop.

questions.question: What does Navigator.pushNamedAndRemoveUntil do?
options: Pushes a named route and removes all previous routes until a condition is met
Pushes a route and then removes it
Removes routes until a condition and then pushes a new one
questions.explanation: It pushes a new route and removes routes from the stack until the predicate returns true.

questions.question: To show a dialog as a route, you can use _____.
questions.explanation: showDialog is a convenience method that pushes a dialog route.

questions.question: You can use WillPopScope to intercept the back button press.
questions.explanation: WillPopScope allows you to handle back button events.

questions.question: What is the difference between push and pushReplacement?
options: push adds a new route; pushReplacement replaces the current one
push is for named routes only
pushReplacement can't be used with named routes
questions.explanation: pushReplacement removes the current route and adds the new one, so you can't go back.

questions.question: The widget that provides the navigation stack is called _____.
questions.explanation: Navigator is the widget that manages routes.

questions.question: You can have multiple Navigators in an app (nested navigation).
questions.explanation: Yes, for example, a bottom navigation bar can have its own nested Navigator.

questions.question: How do you access the Navigator's state from a context?
questions.explanation: Navigator.of(context) returns the NavigatorState.

questions.question: To pass data to a screen via a constructor, you define a _____ parameter.
questions.explanation: Typically, you pass data via final fields in the destination widget's constructor.
